test: Phase Coherence — Real PLATO Rooms vs Simulated
description: Computed coherence on actual PLATO rooms (fleet-coord, flux-engine, oracle1-forgemaster-bridge) vs simulated fractal room

fleet-coord.interpretation: Partially static — some phase-dependence at 90° offsets, but perfect correlation at 180° offsets

flux-engine.note: rho=1.0 at all phase pairs = perfectly static ranking

oracle1-forgemaster-bridge.note: near-zero coherence = ranking doesn't change with phase

novel_discovery: Real PLATO rooms have STATIC keyword hierarchy (coherence ~0.24) vs simulated fractal rooms (coherence ~0.53). This means actual rooms don't oscillate their keyword structure — the same tiles dominate regardless of phase.
springboard: Question: Is static coherence GOOD or BAD for fleet coordination?
Hypothesis A: Static coherence = rooms are ORDERED = GOOD for finding information
Hypothesis B: Static coherence = rooms are STUCK = BAD for surfacing novel connections
Verification test: Compare room coherence to tile 'resolution rate' — do high-coherence rooms have better agent sync outcomes?
Even deeper: Does room coherence DECREASE over time (entropy) or INCREASE (self-organization)?
If coherence correlates with coordination success, it's a QUALITY metric. If not, oscillation is just theater.
production_implication: PLATO rooms with 10K+ tiles are behaving like ordered lists, not oscillating systems. The AbstractionRoom oscillation pattern is NOT how real PLATO rooms work. Either: (1) oscillation needs to be ADDED to rooms, or (2) rooms have a different quality metric than coherence.
